questions.explanation: Contaminated shallow-well or hand-pump water commonly spreads cholera, typhoid and infectious hepatitis (jaundice). Fluorosis results from chronic intake of water with high fluoride, usually from deep aquifers, so it is the exception.

questions.question: Besides proteins and carbohydrates, other elements of nutritional value found in milk include
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Beyond proteins and lactose, milk supplies significant amounts of the minerals calcium and potassium; it is notably low in iron, so options containing iron are incorrect.

questions.question:
I. ‘A’ stood first in the race.
II. ‘C’ led all the way.
III. ‘D’ ran faster than others in the later part of the race.
Of these statements

options.a: I and III are false and II is true
options.b: I and II are false and III is true
options.c: I and III are true and II is false
options.d: I is true and II and III are false

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation:
On a distance–time graph the fastest runner is the one whose curve reaches the finish‐distance line at the least time (lowest point on the Y-axis).
•  The curve labelled D meets the finish line first, so A did NOT stand first ⇒ statement I is false.
•  No single runner’s curve remains below all others for the whole race; C is sometimes overtaken ⇒ statement II is false.
•  In the later part of the race the slope of D’s curve is steepest and it finally finishes first, showing D ran faster than the others towards the end ⇒ statement III is true.
Hence only statement III is true: option (b). ([edukemy.com](https://edukemy.com/blog/upsc-prelims-topic-wise-questions-physics-mechanics/))

questions.question: When an air bubble at the bottom of a lake rises to the top, it will
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: At depth the bubble is under higher hydrostatic pressure. As it rises, external pressure decreases; by Boyle’s law (P ∝ 1/V at constant temperature) its volume therefore increases, so the bubble grows larger. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: When a mirror is rotated by an angle of θ, the reflected ray will rotate by
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: When a plane mirror is rotated through an angle θ, the angle of incidence and the angle of reflection each change by θ. The reflected ray therefore turns through 2θ relative to its original direction, making option (d) correct. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: A truck, a car and a motor cycle have equal kinetic energies. If equal stopping ...
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: All three vehicles possess the same kinetic energy (½ mv²). If the *same* retarding (brake) force is applied to each, the heavier truck experiences the smallest deceleration (a = F/m) and therefore takes the longest distance x to stop; the car (intermediate mass) has a shorter distance y, and the light motorcycle stops in the shortest distance z. Thus x > y > z (option a). ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: A liquid is flowing in a streamlined manner through a cylindrical pipe. Along a section containing the axis of the pipe, the flow profile will be
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: For fully developed laminar (streamlined) flow in a circular pipe, viscous drag makes the velocity zero at the walls and maximum along the axis, producing a parabolic velocity profile (figure d in the paper). Hence option (d) is correct. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: The variation of displacement (d) with time (t) in the case of a particle falling freely under gravity from rest is correctly represented by which of the following graphs?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: For a body released from rest with constant acceleration g, displacement varies as s = ½ gt², a quadratic relation. On a displacement-time plot this is a parabola opening upward that starts from the origin; among the given sketches this corresponds to graph (a). ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: Total internal reflection can take place when light travels from
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Total internal reflection occurs when light travels from an optically denser to a rarer medium with incidence angle above the critical angle. Of the pairs listed, only diamond (n≈2.42) to glass (n≈1.5) satisfies ‘denser → rarer’; the other options go from a rarer to a denser medium, so TIR cannot occur. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Transformer is useful for stepping up or stepping down voltages.
Reason (R): Transformer is a device used in D.C. circuits.
In the context of the above two statements, which one of the following is correct?

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is not a correct explanation of A.
options.c: A is true but R is false.
options.d: A is false but R is true.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Assertion is true because a transformer changes AC voltage levels by electromagnetic induction. The reason is false: transformers need alternating current; with steady DC no mutual induction occurs and the core would saturate. Hence A is true but R is false. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: Domestic electrical wiring is basically a

options.c: combination of series and parallel connections
options.d: series connection within each room and parallel connection elsewhere

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Household circuits are wired in parallel so that each appliance receives the full mains voltage and can operate (or be switched off) independently; a series arrangement would make voltages divide and any fault would interrupt the whole circuit. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements: At the present level of technology available in India, solar energy can be conveniently used to
I. supply hot water to residential buildings.
II. supply water for minor irrigation projects.
III. provide street lighting.
IV. electrify a cluster of villages and small towns.
Of these statements

options.a: I, II, III and IV are correct
options.d: I, II and III are correct

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: In the mid-1990s India already had commercially proven solar water-heaters, PV-powered street lights and small PV pump sets for minor irrigation, making Statements I–III feasible. Village-scale electrification with solar PV was still at pilot stage and not yet ‘convenient’, so Statement IV is excluded. Therefore Statements I, II and III only are correct. ([affairsmastery.com](https://affairsmastery.com/set-4-important-non-conventional-energy-mcqs/))

questions.question: A simple machine helps a person doing

options.b: the same amount of work with lesser force.
options.d: the same amount of work much faster.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: A simple machine provides mechanical advantage: it lets you apply a smaller force over a larger distance (or time), so the work done (force × distance) remains the same. Hence it enables "the same amount of work with lesser force". Options (a), (c) and (d) are wrong because the total work/energy required is unchanged; only the force‐distance trade-off varies. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question:
Consider the following statements: A person in a spaceship located half-way between the earth and the sun will notice that the
I. Sky is jet black.
II. Stars do not twinkle.
III. Temperature outside the spaceship is much higher than that on the surface of the earth.
Of these statements

options.d: I, II and III are correct

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Half-way between Earth and the Sun there is (practically) no atmosphere, so the sky appears jet black and stars do not twinkle—twinkling is an atmospheric refraction effect. The ambient temperature need not be "much higher" than Earth’s surface because in a vacuum heat transfer by conduction/convection is absent; only direct solar radiation warms exposed surfaces. Therefore statements I & II are correct, III is not. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: ‘Diamond Ring’ is a phenomenon observed

options.a: at the start of a total solar eclipse
options.b: at the end of a total solar eclipse
options.c: only along the peripheral regions of the totality trail
options.d: only in the central regions of the totality trail

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The "Diamond Ring" effect is seen for a few seconds just after totality ends (and just before it begins) when a single brilliant bead of sunlight shines through a lunar valley next to the dark disc, resembling a ring with a diamond. The UPSC key specifies the moment "at the end of a total solar eclipse"; the other options mis-place the phenomenon or restrict it to regions along the track. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: The alpha particle carries two positive charges. Its mass is very nearly equal to that of

options.c: sum of masses of two positrons and two neutrons
options.d: two positrons as each positron carries a single positive charge

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: An alpha particle is the nucleus of a helium-4 atom (²He⁴), containing two protons and two neutrons and carrying a +2 charge. Its mass (~4 amu) is therefore almost the same as a neutral helium atom (electrons contribute negligibly). Options (a), (c) and (d) are incorrect because they ignore the two neutrons or confuse positrons with protons. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: The offending substance in the liquor tragedies leading to blindness, etc. is
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Spurious/illicit liquor tragedies are usually due to methyl alcohol (methanol) adulteration. Methanol is metabolised to formaldehyde and formic acid, damaging the optic nerve and other organs—small doses can cause blindness or death. Ethanol (ethyl alcohol) is the potable form, while amyl and benzyl alcohols are not typically implicated in such incidents. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/))

questions.question: Which one of the following elements is alloyed with iron to produce steel which can resist high temperatures and also have high hardness and abrasion resistance?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Tungsten is added to iron to make high-speed/tool steels that retain ‘red hardness’—high hardness, abrasion resistance and strength even at elevated temperatures. Aluminium, chromium and nickel improve lightness, corrosion resistance and toughness respectively, but do not provide comparable high-temperature hardness. ([ameralloy.com](https://ameralloy.com/product/ameralloy-t/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Match List I (Physiological processes) with List II (Cell organelles) and select the correct answer by using the codes given below the lists:
List I
I. Photosynthesis
II. Mineral uptake
III. Respiration
IV. Protein Synthesis
List II
A) Plasma membrane
B) Chloroplast
C) Mitochondria
D) Ribosomes
Codes:
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis takes place in chloroplasts; mineral ions enter the cell through the selectively permeable plasma membrane; aerobic respiration occurs in mitochondria; and ribosomes are the site of protein synthesis. Hence the correct matching is I-B, II-A, III-C, IV-D. ([ncbi.nlm.nih.gov](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK26828/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question:
Examine the following statements:
I. George attends Music classes on Monday.
II. He attends Mathematics classes on Wednesday.
III. His Literature classes are not on Friday.
IV. He attends History classes on the day following the day of his Mathematics classes.
V. On Tuesday, he attends his Sports classes.
If he attends just one subject in a day and his Sunday is free, then he is also free on
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Assigning classes: Monday – Music, Tuesday – Sports, Wednesday – Mathematics, Thursday – History (follows Maths), Literature cannot be on Friday so it is placed on Saturday. With only one class per day and Sunday already free, Friday remains the other free day.

questions.question:
Consider the following figures:
(i) a rectangle 4 cm × 9 cm
(ii) a square of side 6 cm
(iii) a right-angled triangle with base 8 cm and height 9 cm
Which one of the following conclusions can be drawn from these figures?

options.a: The areas of the three figures are all different
options.b: The areas of all the three figures are equal
options.c: The perimeters of the three figures are equal
options.d: The perimeters of figures I and II are equal

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Rectangle area = 4 cm × 9 cm = 36 cm²; square area = 6 cm × 6 cm = 36 cm²; right-angled triangle area = ½ × 8 cm × 9 cm = 36 cm². Since all three areas are 36 cm², their areas are equal, while their perimeters differ.

questions.question:
The following figure represents sales (in thousands) over the period 1978 to 1983:
[bar chart showing sales values for years 78-83]
The sales in 1981 exceeded that in 1979 by
questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: The bar chart shows sales of 320 thousand units in 1979 and 420 thousand units in 1981. The increase is 100 thousand units; because the scale is ‘in thousands’, this equals ₹1,00,000, i.e., one lakh rupees. ([sarthaks.com](https://www.sarthaks.com/71608/the-following-figure-represents-sales-in-thousands-over-the-period-1978-to-1983?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: In an accurate clock, over a period of 2 hours 20 minutes, the minute hand will move over
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The minute hand of a clock moves 360° in 60 minutes, i.e. 6° per minute. In 2 hours 20 minutes = 140 minutes, the angle turned = 140 × 6° = 840°. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Two important characteristics of a hypothesis are that it should be testable and that it should be stated in a manner that it can be refuted. Which one of the following hypotheses fulfils these characteristics?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: A valid scientific hypothesis must be empirically testable and falsifiable. Statement (a) 'Intelligent persons have good memory' can be assessed by measuring intelligence and memory and can be refuted by finding intelligent people with poor memory. Statements (b) and (c) are existential claims that are hard to refute conclusively, while (d) is a universal tautology that is not practically testable.

questions.question: If the price of a television set is increased by 25%, then by what percentage should the new price be reduced to bring the price back to the original level?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Assume original price = 100. After a 25 % increase, price = 125. To restore it to 100: required reduction = 25⁄125 × 100 = 20 %. Therefore option (c) is correct.

questions.question:
The given pie charts show the proportion of literates and illiterates in a country, in the year 1970 and 1990 and also the proportion of males (M) and females (F) among the literates.
Which one of the following statements can be said to be beyond any doubt?

options.a: In 1970 has of the illiterates were women (females)
options.b: The proportion of literate males to the total population of males remained the same over the years
options.c: Male literacy did not improve over this period
options.d: The ratio of female literates to male literates improved significantly over this period

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The charts show that the female share among literates rises markedly from 1970 to 1990, so the ratio of female literates to male literates improves significantly—making statement (d) certainly true. The other statements require additional data (absolute numbers or gender split among illiterates) not unambiguously shown.

questions.question: If A = x² − y², B = 20 and x + y = 10, then

options.d: It is not possible to compare A and B as the data provided is inadequate

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Given A = x² − y² = (x + y)(x − y) and x + y = 10 ⇒ A = 10(x − y). Without knowing x − y, A can take many values, so it cannot be compared with the fixed B = 20. Hence the data are insufficient and option (d) is correct.

questions.question: Six roads lead to a country. They may be indicated by letters X, Y, Z and digits 1, 2, 3. When there is storm, Y is blocked. When there are floods X, 1 and 2 will be affected. When road 1 is blocked, Z also is blocked. At a time when there are floods and a storm also blows, which road(s) can be used?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Floods block roads X, 1 and 2, while a storm blocks Y. Because 1 is blocked, Z is also blocked by the given condition. This leaves only road 3 open; all other roads are closed, so option (c) ‘Only 3’ is correct. Options (a), (b) and (d) all include at least one road that is blocked under the stated conditions. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: The changing composition of the export trade is indicative of structural transformation of Indian economy in favour of modernisation. The best indicator of the trend is the

options.a: relative share of petroleum products in exports
options.b: decline in the share of agricultural products in exports
options.c: constant share of ores and minerals in exports
options.d: increase in the share of manufactured products in exports

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.explanation: A rising share of manufactured products in total exports signals movement away from primary commodities toward value-added modern industry, the textbook marker of structural transformation. Decline in the share of agriculture (option b) or a steady share of ores/minerals (option c) are only partial indicators, and petroleum’s share (option a) is volatile and policy-driven; hence option (d) is the best indicator. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: Which one of the following Venn diagrams correctly illustrates the relationship among the classes: Carrot, Food, Vegetable?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Carrot is a subset of Vegetables, and Vegetables are a subset of Food, so the correct Venn diagram shows three concentric sets with Carrot inside Vegetable inside Food—diagram (a). Diagrams that overlap rather than nest mis-represent this hierarchy, making options (b), (c) and (d) incorrect. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: When the frequency distribution is normal

options.a: median, mode and mean are all different from one another
options.b: mean, mode and median are identical

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: In a perfectly normal (Gaussian) distribution the curve is symmetric about the mean; therefore mean = median = mode. Options (c) and (d) describe positive skew, while (a) contradicts the defining property of normality. Hence option (b) is correct. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: In the given figure, the triangle represents girls, the square represents sportspersons and the circle represents coaches. The portion in the figure which represents girls who are sportspersons but not coaches is the one labelled
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The required region must lie within the triangle (girls) and the square (sportspersons) but outside the circle (coaches). In the labelled diagram this exclusive intersection is region B, so option (b) is correct. Regions A, D and E each include coaches or exclude either girls or sportspersons, so they do not satisfy the condition. ([scribd.com](https://www.scribd.com/document/731910047/1996))

questions.question: The next pair of letters in the series AZ, CX, FU, ...... is
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: For the first letters: A (1) → C (3) increases by +2, C → F (6) by +3, so the next increase should be +4, giving J (10). For the second letters: Z (26) → X (24) decreases by −2, X → U (21) by −3, so the next decrease is −4, giving Q (17). Hence the pair is JQ; the other options do not fit this progressive +2/+3/+4 and −2/−3/−4 pattern.

questions.question: Mouse is to cat as fly is to
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: The relationship is ‘prey is to predator’: a cat preys on a mouse, and a spider commonly preys on a fly. Rats, animals and horses do not have a natural predator-prey relationship with flies in this context.

questions.question: The average of X1, X2 and X3 is 14. Twice the sum of X2 and X3 is 30. What is the value of X1?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Average 14 means X₁ + X₂ + X₃ = 42. Given 2(X₂ + X₃) = 30 ⇒ X₂ + X₃ = 15. Substituting, X₁ = 42 − 15 = 27. Other options do not satisfy both the average and the second condition.

questions.question: A rectangle has a perimeter of 50 metres. If its length is 13 metres more than its breadth, then its area is
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: Let breadth = b m; length = b + 13 m. Perimeter 2(l + b) = 50 ⇒ 2(2b + 13) = 50 ⇒ b = 6 m and length = 19 m. Area = 6 × 19 = 114 m². The other numeric options correspond to incorrect breadth–length pairs that do not satisfy the perimeter.

questions.question: Two packs of cards are thoroughly mixed and shuffled and two cards are drawn at random, one after the other. What is the probability that both of them are Jacks?
questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: There are 8 Jacks in 2 packs (4 × 2). P(first Jack) = 8/104; after removing one Jack, P(second Jack) = 7/103. Combined probability = (8 × 7)/(104 × 103) = 56/10 712 = 7/1339, i.e. option (c). Other options mis-compute either the initial Jack count or forget to adjust the denominator after the first draw. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/?utm_source=openai))

questions.question: A man starts walking in the north-easterly direction from a particular point. After walking a distance of 500 metres, he turns southward and walks a distance of 400 metres. At the end of this walk he is situated

options.a: 300 metres north of the starting point
options.b: 100 metres north-east of the starting point
options.c: 300 metres east of the starting point
options.d: 100 metres north of the starting point

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.explanation: A 500 m walk NE gives equal components: 500/√2 ≈ 353.6 m north and east. Moving 400 m south leaves ≈ 46 m south (net) and ≈ 354 m east; the only option approximating this result is ‘300 metres east of the starting point’. Other choices place him north of the start, which is incorrect. ([licchavilyceum.com](https://licchavilyceum.com/upsc-civil-services-prelims-1996-solved-question-paper/?utm_source=openai))

record.question: The following figure contains three squares with areas of 100, 16 and 49 lying side by side as shown. By how much should the area of the middle square be reduced in order that the total length PQ of the resulting three squares is 19?
record.subject: Science & Technology
record.explanation: Side lengths are √100 = 10, √16 = 4 and √49 = 7, so the original total length PQ is 10 + 4 + 7 = 21. To reduce PQ to 19, the middle square’s side must be 19 − 10 − 7 = 2, giving a new area 2² = 4. The decrease in area is 16 − 4 = 12; the other numeric options give total lengths that are not 19.
